_meta.description: Few-shot example dataset for an AI-provider taxonomy tracker. Built from scratch, current as of 2026-06-15. Records are chosen to teach edge cases and granularity patterns, not to be exhaustive.

capabilities.name: Flagship model
capabilities.description: Each provider's most capable general-purpose model.

capabilities.name: Consumer chat assistant
capabilities.description: Primary conversational app for end users across web, desktop, and mobile.

capabilities.name: Agentic coding
capabilities.description: Autonomous or assisted software development across terminal, IDE, and cloud surfaces.

capabilities.name: Remote / async agent control
capabilities.description: Starting, monitoring, and directing an agent (esp. a coding agent) from a remote device or asynchronously.

capabilities.name: Browser / computer-use agent
capabilities.description: An agent that drives a browser or computer on the user's behalf to complete multi-step web tasks.

capabilities.name: Knowledge-work / research agent
capabilities.description: Non-developer agent for documents, analysis, and research grounded in user sources.

capabilities.name: Agent-building SDK / framework
capabilities.description: Developer toolkit for building, orchestrating, and deploying custom agents.

capabilities.name: Enterprise agent platform / deployment
capabilities.description: Managed platform for building, deploying, governing, and running agents and models at enterprise scale.

capabilities.name: Image / video generation
capabilities.description: First-party models or products that generate images or video.

capabilities.name: Unclassified (intake)
capabilities.description: Holding bucket for newly discovered offerings/features not yet mapped to a capability. The discovery pipeline lands items here as candidates; triage assigns the real capability.

products.name: Claude model family
products.scope_note: Tiered family. Children show the MODEL_FAMILY -> MODEL hierarchy: how to model tiers without flattening them.

products.name: Claude Opus 4.8
products.scope_note: Flagship tier. A newer Fable 5 sits in a tier above Opus.

products.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
products.scope_note: Balanced tier (not the flagship), hence relation 'partial' to the flagship-model capability.

products.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
products.scope_note: Fast/cheap tier.

products.name: GPT-5.4
products.scope_note: Coding-tuned variants (GPT-5.4 for Codex, Codex-Spark) ship on a separate cadence. A model_family node could be added to mirror the Claude pattern.

products.name: Gemini 3.5 Pro
products.scope_note: Gemini 3.5 Flash is the fast / agentic-coding tier; Gemini Omni is the newer video model.

lifecycle.note: Gemini 3.5 family and Omni unveiled at Google I/O 2026.

products.name: ChatGPT
products.scope_note: PENDING CONSOLIDATION: slated to merge with Codex and the Atlas browser into a single desktop 'superapp'; no firm date.

lifecycle.note: ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas to combine into one superapp.

products.name: Gemini app
products.scope_note: Being repositioned from chat assistant toward an always-on, task-capable personal agent (Gemini Agent).

products.name: Claude Code
products.scope_note: Dedicated coding agent. Narrower than OpenAI Codex, which has expanded into general agent tasks. Has child features (see Remote Control).

products.name: Claude Code Remote Control
products.scope_note: FEATURE-OF-A-PRODUCT pattern: an encrypted bridge to drive a local Claude Code session from the Claude mobile app/web; code stays on the local machine. Research preview. Tracked because remote/async agent control is comparable across providers.

products.name: Codex
products.scope_note: SCOPE MISMATCH: pivoting from a coding tool into a general-purpose agent platform, so it spans more than agentic coding. Has family members (Codex CLI, Codex desktop).

lifecycle.note: 'Codex For Almost Everything' repositions it as a general agent platform.

products.name: Codex CLI
products.scope_note: SUB-PRODUCT pattern: terminal client in the Codex family (parent_id -> Codex). Operates independently for now despite the superapp consolidation.

products.name: Codex Cloud (remote/async runs)
products.scope_note: CANDIDATE (discovery pipeline): Codex Cloud runs async tasks that can be kicked off and checked remotely. Whether a direct mobile 'remote control' equivalent to Claude Code's exists is unconfirmed — needs verification before promotion to 'confirmed'.

products.name: Antigravity 2.0
products.scope_note: Agent-first development platform (desktop app + SDK). Connects to AI Studio, Firebase, Android, and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

products.name: Antigravity CLI
products.scope_note: Go rewrite that replaces Gemini CLI.

products.name: Jules
products.scope_note: MULTI-CAPABILITY: an asynchronous coding agent (a slice of agentic-coding) that is also Google's analog for remote/async agent control.

lifecycle.note: Jules V2 (Project Jitro) presented at I/O 2026.

products.name: Gemini CLI
products.scope_note: SUNSET: being retired for individual/consumer tiers; enterprise Gemini Code Assist access is retained.

lifecycle.note: Migration to Antigravity CLI announced at I/O 2026.
Stops serving requests for AI Pro, Ultra, and free-tier users.

products.name: Claude in Chrome
products.scope_note: Browsing agent (beta).

products.name: Atlas
products.scope_note: AI browser; slated to fold into the unified ChatGPT/Codex superapp.

products.name: Project Mariner
products.scope_note: DISCONTINUED as a standalone product; capabilities folded into Gemini Agent and Chrome auto-browse.

lifecycle.note: Shut down as standalone; tech absorbed into Gemini Agent + Chrome auto-browse.

products.name: Gemini Agent
products.scope_note: Absorbed Project Mariner's web-task capabilities; can perform tasks such as archiving emails and booking hotels.

products.name: Claude Cowork
products.scope_note: Agentic knowledge-work app for non-developers; can use Claude in Excel/PowerPoint/Chrome as tools.

products.name: NotebookLM
products.scope_note: Research/notebook assistant grounded in user-provided sources.

products.name: Workspace Agents (in ChatGPT)
products.scope_note: FEATURE-OF-A-PRODUCT: natural-language knowledge-work agents living inside ChatGPT, recommended as the no-code successor to the retired Agent Builder.

products.name: Claude Agent SDK
products.scope_note: Pairs with MCP (Model Context Protocol), the interop standard Anthropic created and that all three providers now support.

products.name: Agents SDK
products.scope_note: Code-first path. The no-code Agent Builder and Evals (part of AgentKit) are winding down - available until 2026-11-30.

lifecycle.note: Agent Builder & Evals winding down; Agents SDK recommended for code workflows.

products.name: Agent Development Kit (ADK)
products.scope_note: Multi-language agent framework (ADK 1.0). Also bundled inside the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

products.name: Vertex AI
products.scope_note: RENAME LINEAGE: the long-standing enterprise ML/AI platform, renamed to Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform in April 2026. Kept as a record so the old name resolves to the new one.

lifecycle.note: Rebranded as Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform at Cloud Next '26.

products.name: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
products.scope_note: Build/scale/govern/optimize agents; evolution of Vertex AI. Includes Agent Studio (low-code), Agent Gateway, ADK, and Model Garden (200+ models, incl. Claude). Note brand ambiguity vs. 'Gemini Enterprise' (the workplace front-door).

lifecycle.note: Announced at Cloud Next '26 as the evolution of Vertex AI.

products.name: Gemini Enterprise (workplace)
products.scope_note: BRAND AMBIGUITY: 'Gemini Enterprise' is the workplace front-door agentic system, distinct from the similarly named 'Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform' (the developer platform). Same brand family, two capabilities - a case the schema must disambiguate.

products.name: Claude on third-party platforms
products.scope_note: CROSS-PROVIDER DEPLOYMENT: Anthropic has no first-party enterprise agent platform of its own. Claude models are offered INSIDE competitors' platforms - Google's Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (Model Garden) and AWS Bedrock. A provider can be present in a capability only via others' infrastructure.

products.name: (none)
products.scope_note: ABSENCE RECORD: Anthropic has no first-party image or video generation product. Modeled explicitly so the app renders a sourced gap rather than a missing/null cell.

products.name: Sora
products.scope_note: Video generation. DALL-E covers image generation under the same provider.

products.name: Veo
products.scope_note: Video generation (Veo 3 in the API). Imagen covers image generation; Gemini Omni is the newer video model.
